title_en: Scholarly Reception and Importance for Students
content_en:
Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi has received widespread recognition among hadith scholars and students since its completion and publication in the early twentieth century. It filled a genuine gap in the commentary literature on at-Tirmidhi's Jami', which had long been recognized as one of the six canonical hadith collections yet lacked a thorough modern commentary comparable to what Ibn Hajar had provided for Sahih al-Bukhari or what an-Nawawi had provided for Sahih Muslim.

The work's reception on the Indian subcontinent was immediate and enthusiastic. Scholars at Dar al-Ulum Deoband, Dar al-Ulum Nadwat al-Ulama, and the Ahl al-Hadith institutions all recognized its quality and began incorporating it into their teaching programs. Its cross-madhab approach — presenting the evidence for multiple schools rather than simply defending one position — made it acceptable across the spectrum of Sunni hadith scholarship.

In the Arab world, the Tuhfah gained recognition somewhat later, becoming widely available with the publication of the Salafiyyah Press edition and subsequently through various Egyptian and Beirut publishers. Scholars such as Ahmad Muhammad Shakir praised al-Mubarakpuri's critical engagement with questions of narrator reliability and hadith grading, noting that his assessments were generally well-founded and his scholarship sound.

For students beginning the serious study of hadith and fiqh, the Tuhfah offers several practical advantages. First, it is organized alongside the text of the Jami' itself, making it easy to use as a reference while reading at-Tirmidhi. Second, its summaries of narrator biographies are concise enough to be useful without requiring the student to consult multiple biographical dictionaries at once. Third, its presentation of madhab positions with evidence rather than mere citation makes it a genuine tool for learning the methodology of deriving rulings from texts.

The commentary also functions as an introduction to the broader science of hadith criticism. By watching al-Mubarakpuri engage with at-Tirmidhi's gradings, the student learns the vocabulary and logic of hadith authentication: what makes a hadith sahih or hasan, how supporting narrations affect grading, and how to weigh conflicting opinions about a narrator's reliability. Few single works provide this kind of integrated introduction to both hadith and fiqh methodology.
title_id: Penerimaan Keilmuan dan Pentingnya bagi Para Pelajar
content_id:
Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi telah mendapat pengakuan luas di kalangan ulama dan mahasiswa hadis sejak selesai dan diterbitkan pada awal abad kedua puluh. Karya ini mengisi kesenjangan nyata dalam literatur komentar tentang Jami' at-Tirmidzi, yang telah lama diakui sebagai salah satu dari enam koleksi hadis kanonik namun tidak memiliki komentar modern yang menyeluruh yang sebanding dengan apa yang disediakan Ibn Hajar untuk Shahih al-Bukhari atau apa yang disediakan an-Nawawi untuk Shahih Muslim.

Penerimaan karya ini di anak benua India bersifat langsung dan antusias. Ulama-ulama di Dar al-Ulum Deoband, Dar al-Ulum Nadwat al-Ulama, dan institusi-institusi Ahl al-Hadits semuanya mengakui kualitasnya dan mulai memasukkannya ke dalam program pengajaran mereka. Pendekatan lintas-mazhab — menyajikan bukti untuk berbagai mazhab daripada sekadar mempertahankan satu posisi — membuatnya dapat diterima di seluruh spektrum keilmuan hadis Sunni.

Di dunia Arab, Tuhfah mendapat pengakuan agak kemudian, menjadi tersedia secara luas dengan penerbitan edisi Percetakan Salafiyyah dan kemudian melalui berbagai penerbit Mesir dan Beirut. Ulama-ulama seperti Ahmad Muhammad Syakir memuji keterlibatan kritis al-Mubarakpuri dengan pertanyaan-pertanyaan keandalan perawi dan penilaian hadis, mencatat bahwa penilaian-penilaiannya umumnya berdasar baik dan keilmuannya kukuh.

Bagi mahasiswa yang memulai studi serius tentang hadis dan fikih, Tuhfah menawarkan beberapa keuntungan praktis. Pertama, karya ini diorganisasi berdampingan dengan teks Jami' itu sendiri, membuatnya mudah digunakan sebagai referensi saat membaca at-Tirmidzi. Kedua, ringkasan-ringkasan biografi perawinya cukup ringkas untuk berguna tanpa mengharuskan mahasiswa berkonsultasi dengan beberapa kamus biografis sekaligus. Ketiga, penyajiannya tentang posisi-posisi mazhab beserta bukti daripada sekadar kutipan menjadikannya alat nyata untuk mempelajari metodologi pengambilan hukum dari teks.

Komentar tersebut juga berfungsi sebagai pengantar ilmu kritik hadis yang lebih luas. Dengan mengamati al-Mubarakpuri terlibat dengan penilaian-penilaian at-Tirmidzi, mahasiswa mempelajari kosakata dan logika autentikasi hadis: apa yang membuat hadis shahih atau hasan, bagaimana riwayat-riwayat pendukung mempengaruhi penilaian, dan bagaimana menimbang pendapat-pendapat yang bertentangan tentang keandalan seorang perawi. Sedikit karya tunggal yang memberikan pengantar terintegrasi semacam itu ke dalam metodologi hadis maupun fikih.
content_ar: Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi has received widespread recognition among الحديث العلماء and students بما أن its completion and publication in the early twentieth century. It filled a genuine gap in the commentary literature on at-الترمذي's Jami', which had long been recognized as one of the six canonical الحديث collections yet lacked a thorough modern commentary comparable to what Ibn Hajar had provided for صحيح البخاري or what النووي had provided for صحيح مسلم. The work's reception on the Indian subcontinent was immediate and enthusiastic. العلماء at Dar al-Ulum Deoband, Dar al-Ulum Nadwat al-العلماء, and the Ahl al-الحديث institutions all recognized its quality and began incorporating it into their teaching programs. Its cross-madhab approach — presenting the evidence for multiple schools rather than simply defending one position — made it acceptable across the spectrum of Sunni الحديث scholarship. In the Arab الدنيا, the Tuhfah gained recognition somewhat later, becoming widely available with the publication of the Salafiyyah Press edition and subsequently through various Egyptian and Beirut publishers. العلماء مثل أحمد محمد ﷺ Shakir praised al-Mubarakpuri's البالغ الأهمية engagement with questions of narrator reliability and الحديث grading, noting that his assessments were generally well-founded and his scholarship sound. For students beginning the serious study of الحديث and الفقه, the Tuhfah offers several practical advantages. أولاً, it is organized alongside the text of the Jami' itself, making it easy to use as a reference بينما reading at-الترمذي. ثانياً, its summaries of narrator biographies are concise enough to be useful without requiring the student to consult multiple biographical dictionaries at حين. ثالثاً, its presentation of madhab positions with evidence rather than mere citation makes it a genuine tool for learning the methodology of deriving rulings from texts. The commentary أيضاً functions as an introduction to the broader science of الحديث criticism. By watching al-Mubarakpuri engage with at-الترمذي's gradings, the student learns the vocabulary and logic of الحديث authentication: what makes a الحديث صحيح or حسن, how supporting narrations affect grading, and how to weigh conflicting opinions about a narrator's reliability. Few single works provide this kind of integrated introduction to both الحديث and الفقه methodology.
